task_context.question: Who wrote the debut song on the debut album of the artist who also released the album "I'm Still Here"?
task_context.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Who wrote the debut song on the debut album of the artist who also released the album "I'm Still Here"?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
Evidence found:
- Evans Blue — [1]: '"Cold (But I'm Still Here)" is the first single'
- Agnes — [2]: 'The track was written by Jörgen Elofsson'
- Foster & Lloyd — [3]: '"Crazy Over You" is a debut song'
- Esmée Denters — [4]: '"Outta Here" is the title song of the debut studio album'
- Gabriela (singer) — [5]: 'Her self-titled debut album was released in 2001;'
- Sammy Kershaw — [6]: 'from his debut album, "Don't Go Near the Water".'
- Melanie Martinez — [7]: 'this song is also the first official song Melanie Martinez wrote.'

Ambiguities:
- The phrase "I'm Still Here" appears as part of the song title "Cold (But I'm Still Here)" (passage [1]) but no passage shows an album titled "I'm Still Here".
- Multiple "debut song" examples exist (e.g., Foster & Lloyd, Melanie Martinez) but none are linked to an album titled "I'm Still Here".

Bridge / missing fact:
- Missing identity of the artist who released an album titled "I'm Still Here".
- Missing that artist's debut album title and which track is the debut song on that debut album.
- Missing the songwriter credit for that debut song.

Retrieval direction:
- Open "I'm Still Here (album)" (Wikipedia page) or "I'm Still Here (disambiguation)" to identify the album's artist.
- Run the query: '"I'm Still Here" album artist' (search exact phrase).
- After identifying the artist, open that artist's page and their debut album page (e.g., '"[Artist name]" discography' or '"[Artist name]" debut album').
- Also check "Cold (But I'm Still Here)" (passage [1]) to avoid confusing the song title with an album title.
Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets above.

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Agent role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

Pipeline position: after summary1 and before second-hop retrieval. Inputs are the original question and the first-hop intermediate state. Functional responsibility: produce one focused retrieval query that is likely to recover the missing evidence needed by the downstream agents. Query quality is determined by evidence recovery, not by whether the query itself resembles or contains the final answer.

Native output contract: The module must consume `question` and `summary_1` and produce exactly one compact second-hop query field named `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include every distinct entity token visible in the inputs in the single terse query and place at most one neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the targeted relation or attribute (for example: "EntityA EntityB director").
- If a visible disambiguator or attribute token appears adjacent to an entity in the inputs (for example a multiword service name, an explicit page title, a year, or an attribute marker like a birth/producer/service/series cue), include that disambiguator token together with the associated entity in the query rather than dropping or truncating it.
- If `summary_1` contains an explicit page-title token sequence (for example an instruction-like ExactPageTitle or Fetch page title), prefer that exact page-title token sequence as the query value.
- When the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.

retrieved_titles: Guys Do It All the Time
A Girl's Gotta Do (What a Girl's Gotta Do)
retrieved_doc_snippets: Ten Thousand Angels (song) | "Ten Thousand Angels" is a debut song written by Billy Henderson and Steven Dale Jones, and recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released on April 1, 1996 as the first single and title track from the album "Ten Thousand Angels". The song reached #6 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart.

I'm Still Here (album) | I'm Still Here is the fifth and final studio album by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released on March 23, 2010, via Linus Entertainment. The album includes the single "I'm Still Here," as well as re-recordings of her 1996 singles "Guys Do It All the Time" and "Ten Thousand Angels." Mindy performed on "Fox & Friends" to promote the album in April 2010. This is McCready's final release before her death in 2013.

Mindy McCready | Malinda Gayle "Mindy" McCready (November 30, 1975 – February 17, 2013) was an American country music singer. Active from 1995 until her death in 2013, she recorded a total of five studio albums. Her debut album, 1996's "Ten Thousand Angels", was released on BNA Records and was certified 2× Platinum by the RIAA, while 1997's "If I Don't Stay the Night" was certified Gold. 1999's "I'm Not So Tough", her final album for BNA, was less successful, and she left the label. A self-title ...[truncated]

Guys Do It All the Time | "Guys Do It All the Time" is a song recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready and written by Bobby Whiteside and Kim Tribble. The song was released on July 15, 1996, and reached the No. 1 spot on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart on September 14 of that year. It is McCready's first and only Number One hit. The song was the second single from McCready's double-platinum selling debut album "Ten Thousand Angels". A video was also issued of t ...[truncated]

Maybe He'll Notice Her Now | "Maybe He'll Notice Her Now" is a song written by Tim Johnson and recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready featuring Richie McDonald. It was released on January 6, 1997 as the third single from McCready's double-platinum selling album "Ten Thousand Angels". The song reached number 18 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart.

Ten Thousand Angels | Ten Thousand Angels is the 1996 debut album of American country music singer Mindy McCready. It was released on BNA Records and sold two million copies being certified double platinum by the RIAA. The album peaked at #5 on the US country charts, and producing four chart singles on the country charts. The first single, which was the title track, reached #6, and was followed by her only #1 hit, "Guys Do It All the Time". Following this song was "Maybe He'll Notice Her Now", w ...[truncated]

A Girl's Gotta Do (What a Girl's Gotta Do) | "A Girl's Gotta Do (What a Girl's Gotta Do)" is a song written by Robert Byrne and Rick Bowles, and recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released in February 1997 as the fourth single from the album "Ten Thousand Angels". The song reached number 4 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart.
target_state.summary_2: The artist who also released the album "I'm Still Here" is Mindy McCready, and the debut song "Ten Thousand Angels" on her debut album was written by Billy Henderson and Steven Dale Jones.
target_state.answer: Billy Henderson and Steven Dale Jones
